stories.title: US Director of National Intelligence raised issues with spy law Trump is pushing to renew
stories.summary: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard told U.S. President Donald Trump in early February she had reservations about reauthorizing a key spy authority without incorporating reforms to protect Americans’ privacy, according to two people with knowledge of the exchange. She expressed her concerns to Trump during a White House meeting, as the president was still weighing his position, said the two people, who, like others in this report, were granted anonymity to share details of the sensit

stories.title: EU officials arrive in Hungary for high-stakes talks with Magyar’s government
stories.summary: Departing PM Viktor Orbán admits ‘political era has ended’ as EU says ‘clock is ticking’ to resolve important issues EU officials have arrived in Budapest for high-stakes talks aimed at reshaping the bloc’s strained relationship with Hungary, weeks before the new government takes office, as the country’s departing prime minister, Viktor Orbán, admitted a “political era has ended” and suggested he would stay on as leader of his party in his first interview since the election. Speaking to the pro-

stories.title: A decade on from Brexit, Britain still flounders without a place in the world | Letters
stories.summary: Raj Parkash and Chris Manners on the lasting impact of the UK’s decision in 2016 to leave the EU Perhaps the saddest consequence of Brexit is that it has left the UK a profoundly unhappy country ( Ten years after Brexit, this is the UK: a divided nation frozen in time, 9 April ). For Brexit supporters, the dream of a proud, independent Britain able to direct its own destiny in the world is nowhere near fulfilled, as if it ever could be. And they continue to grasp at the nearest proximate cause –

stories.title: Hormuz standoff shows no signs of letting up for vital shipping to resume from Gulf
stories.summary: Maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz remained at minimal levels on Friday resulting in a giant parking lot, as the US-Iran standoff continues with the same cautious approach since Monday.

stories.title: Orbán’s defeat threatens to halt Hungarian support of populist right
stories.summary: Individuals such as Matt Goodwin and Lord Frost benefited from largesse of self-styled ‘illiberal democracy’ UK politics live – latest updates The last 16 years of Viktor Orbán’s rule have been kind to a number of British political figures – from the Tory peer David Frost to Reform UK’s Matt Goodwin and James Orr. All benefited from largesse extended by the self-styled “illiberal democracy” established by the Hungarian leader’s ruling Fidesz party, which took a particular liking for those on the

stories.title: Magyar Péter felhívására lemondott a közmédia kuratóriumának egyik tagja
stories.summary: Silhavy Máté Tibor az ország összes közjogi tisztségviselőjét arra kérte, hogy ne dugják homokba a fejüket.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Nagy Márton tárgyalt Kármán Andrással, megegyeztek az átadás-átvételről
stories.summary: Legkorábban május 7-én megalakulhat az új parlament. A leendő miniszterelnök nagyon szeretne ügyelni arra, hogy az ellenzék jogosítványai biztosítva legyenek. Curtis is megszólalt.

stories.title: Pozitív hangvételűnek nevezte az Egyesült Államokban folytatott tárgyalásokat az ukrán miniszterelnök
stories.summary: Az egyeztetésen Julija Szviridenko az amerikai pénzügyminiszterrel találkozott, és egyebek mellett az Oroszországgal szembeni szankciók fontosságáról esett szó.

stories.title: Egy orosz drón megsértette Románia légterét
stories.summary: Két drónt észleltek a radarok, az egyik rövid időre belépett Románia területére.

stories.title: Lemossák az Árpád hidat
stories.summary: Szombaton a déli, vasárnapig pedig az északi oldalt tisztítja meg a Budapesti Közművek.

stories.title: Kétharmaddal sem olyan egyszerű leváltani Sulyok Tamást, ha nem távozik önként
stories.summary: A megfosztási eljárásban megkerülhetetlen szerepe van az Alkotmánybíróságnak, amelynek a tagjait nem tudja elmozdítani a parlament. Létezik ugyan egy bonyolultabb jogi megoldás, de annak elég rossz lenne az optikája. Magyar Péter szerint a köztársasági elnök megfontolja a távozását, Sulyok nyilvánosan még nem reagált a felszólítására.

stories.title: World-renowned pianist András Schiff to perform in Hungary again after Orbán's downfall
stories.summary: He said that for the first time in sixteen years, he is proud of Hungary and is planning to play before as big an audience as possible.

stories.title: A katolikus és az evangélikus egyház is gratulált Magyar Péter győzelméhez
stories.summary: Mindketten konstruktív és partneri együttműködésre fognak törekedni a Tiszával, írták.

stories.title: Iványi Gábor szerint felelősségre kell vonni Orbán Viktort, különben visszatérhet
stories.summary: Jogszerű eljárás után börtönben lenne a helye a miniszterelnöknek a lelkész szerint.

stories.title: Új korszak, régi kérdés: hogyan gyorsítsuk fel Magyarország gazdasági növekedését?
stories.summary: Az Egyensúly Intézet április 28-án, a G7 Paradigma konferencián mutatja be új szakpolitikai javaslatcsomagját amelynek témája: hogyan állíthatnánk hosszú távú növekedési pályára a magyar gazdaságot? A növekedés eddigi modellje ugyanis minden jel szerint kifulladt. Ha nem sikerül érdemben növelnünk a termelékenységünket, tartós gazdasági pangásra ítéljük magunkat, és esélyünk sem marad kievickélni a legszegényebb uniós országok köréből.

stories.title: Egy, az MBH Bank csapatában tekerő 19 éves magyar kerékpáros bukott meg doppinggal
stories.summary: Makrai Bálint egy versenyen kívüli teszten adott pozitív doppingmintát, szteroidhasználatot mutattak ki nála.

stories.title: A Media Markt tulajdonosa is beszállt egy megfeneklett bálna mentőakciójába, ami lázban tartja a németeket
stories.summary: A Timmy nevű bálnát légpárnákkal próbálják megemelni a Balti-tengeren, miközben politikusok is próbálnak vele népszerűséget szerezni.

stories.title: A Times Hegedűs Zsolt leendő egészségügyi miniszter győzelmi táncáról és terveiről közölt cikket
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt választási győzelmének kihirdetése után breakelő és léggitározó csípő- és térdsebész a brit egészségügyi rendszert bizonyos elemeit tekinti mintának a magyar ellátás reformjához.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint még nincs döntés az oktatási miniszter személyéről
stories.summary: A Tisza elnöke a Parlamentben azt mondta, a jövő héten több miniszteri pozícióval kapcsolatban bejelentést fog tenni.

stories.title: Sűrű napja van a Molnak és az OTP-nek, emelkedéssel indult a nap a magyar tőzsdén
stories.summary: Az OTP ma tartja közgyűlését, Csányi Sándor először szólalt meg a vasárnapi országgyűlési választás után.

stories.title: A Tisza Párt alatt is folytatódik a védett ár egyre vészjóslóbb rendszere
stories.summary: Nem lett volna népszerű intézkedés a védett árak azonnali kivezetése a megválasztott kormánytól, de előbb-utóbb, sokkal inkább előbb, mint utóbb, meg kell majd lépni. Most annyiban enyhült a nyomás, hogy Szlovákiából újra lehet dízelt importálni.

stories.title: Az RMDSZ alelnöke interjúban magyarázta pártja és a Fidesz szoros viszonyának okait
stories.summary: Székely István szerint a Fidesz vereségét részben az okozta, hogy elvesztette mozgalmi jellegét, pártjának pedig megújulás helyett folyamatos korrekcióra van szüksége.

stories.title: A finn elnök a magyar választásról: Hazudnék, ha azt mondanám, hogy nem mosolyogtam az elmúlt négy napban
stories.summary: Alexander Stubb finn köztársasági elnök megkönnyebbült a magyar választások eredménye miatt.

stories.title: Az Integritás Hatóság elnöke nem ugrana el a több feladat elől, sőt vannak javaslatai
stories.summary: Bíró Ferenc belső levele alapján sokat vár a kormányváltástól, a hatóság maga is ötleteket fogalmaz meg Magyar Péter felé, hogyan bővülhetne hasznosan a jogköre.

stories.title: Ezek voltak Nagy Márton miniszter legjobb pillanatai
stories.summary: A Fidesz választási bukása után öt nappal Nagy Márton nemzetgazdasági miniszter bejelentette, hogy visszavonul a politikától. Orbán korábban azt mondta, neki egy szakember kell, akit nem érdekel a politika, a miniszterelnök szerint ezért szoktak is viccelődni Nagy Márton előadásain.

stories.title: Márki-Zay Péter: Nem utaltunk Orbánnak szolidaritási adót, nehogy Tiborczékhoz kerüljön a 263 milliónk!
stories.summary: Az önkormányzatoknak legkésőbb szerdán kellett volna befizetniük a szolidaritási hozzájárulás második részletét.

stories.title: Felpörgeti a kormányalakítást Magyar Péter, lefoglalja a Kossuth teret az alakuló ülés napjára
stories.summary: A Tisza hétvégi napon szeretné megtartani az alakuló ülést, a napján országos rendezvényt tartanának a Kossuth téren. Jövő héten jelenthetik be, ki lesz a Tisza frakcióvezetője és Kövér László utódja. Magyar Péterék több gesztust is tettek az új ellenzéknek.

stories.title: Csányi Sándort a Tisza győzelme nem lepte meg, a kétharmad igen
stories.summary: Az OTP elnöke a bank közgyűlése után arról is beszélt, hogy mit gondol a Tisza gazdaságpolitikusairól, és hogy menne-e még Orbán Viktorral focimeccsre.

stories.title: Sinlaku rips through Northern Mariana Islands as strongest tropical cyclone this year
stories.summary: More than 1,000 people were in shelters across Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands as Sinlaku moved away Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Super Typhoon Sinlaku hammered the Northern Mariana Islands, flipping over cars, toppling utility poles and ripping away tin roofs. Authorities were just beginning to assess the damage left behind by the typhoon, which first hit the islands on Tuesday night local time and continued with a barrage of fierce winds a

stories.title: Hungary’s Péter Magyar says new government could take power at beginning of May
stories.summary: Magyar has called on Orbán's government to act as a caretaker in its final weeks and not to make any decisions that could threaten Hungary's interests or impede the incoming government's work.

stories.title: Humanoid robot chases wild boars in the Polish capital Warsaw
stories.summary: A humanoid robot named Edward Warchocki went viral after chasing wild boars across a parking lot in Warsaw. The Unitree G1 robot has gained attention in recent weeks for its public appearances and interactions. Developed in China, the robot’s personality is a Polish creation.

stories.title: Berlin approves defence initiatives as domestic industry receives drone and frigate deals
stories.summary: New combat drones are planned for use by the so-called Lithuania Brigade, a unit of around 5,000 soldiers who guarantee the defence and security of NATO's eastern flank until the end of 2027.

stories.title: Hungary: What's next for Peter Magyar after election win?
stories.summary: Peter Magyar has promised nothing less than a "system change" in Hungary. The expectations both at home and abroad are huge. DW explores what Magyar is likely to do on the most pressing issues facing Hungary.

stories.title: What next for Hungary as country celebrates Péter Magyar victory over Viktor Orbán?
stories.summary: People in the capital, Budapest, and across Hungary celebrated the victory of the opposition party Tisza, led by Péter Magyar, which has won the parliamentary elections, putting an end to the 16-year rule of the populist Viktor Orbán. The Guardian's Europe reporter Jakub Krupa reports from the Hungarian capital Hungarian opposition ousts Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power Europe live – latest updates Continue reading...

stories.title: Will Orbán’s defeat in Hungary be a turning point for Europe? – The Latest
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule has come to an end after the opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, won the Hungary election by a landslide. Orbán quickly conceded after what he described as a 'painful but unambiguous' result. His defeat will come as a blow to Donald Trump, who has repeatedly endorsed Orbán and whose vice-president, JD Vance, had travelled to Budapest days before the election. All eyes are now on Magyar to see how Hungary’s relationship with the EU and Nato could change after

stories.title: EU applauds Hungary's election result
stories.summary: After Hungary’s opposition ousted Viktor Orban after 16 years in power, hopes rise in Brussels that Budapest may abandon its obstructionist course. What is the EU expecting from Peter Magyar?

stories.title: Hungary: Orban election loss reverberates across Central Europe
stories.summary: Viktor Orban's crushing defeat in the April 12 election will have far-reaching consequences for Europe's political landscape — and especially for neighboring Czechia and Slovakia, which are run by his political allies.

stories.title: I want to reform our country because a strong Germany is a precondition for a strong Europe | Lars Klingbeil
stories.summary: The war in Iran has exposed our dependencies. Europe, including the UK, must be bold about change, so nobody can blackmail us Lars Klingbeil is Germany’s finance minister and vice-chancellor Wars and crises are draining our economies, our sense of security and our emotional wellbeing. They are affecting our daily lives: supply chains are becoming less reliable, energy prices are soaring, and trade dependencies on fossil-fuel energy and critical minerals pose risks to national security. Tariffs,

stories.title: EU eyes tougher penalties for single market rule-breakers
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission is set to impose tougher penalties on countries obstructing trade across the EU, according to a draft document seen by POLITICO.  President Ursula von der Leyen wants to revamp the three-decade-old single market to boost the bloc’s competitiveness vis-à-vis China and the U.S., drawing on the recommendations of former Italian Prime Minister Mario Draghi, who has warned that the EU faces a “ slow agony of decline ” if it fails to take decisive action.

stories.title: Embassy cables detail how Iran war is hurting the US abroad
stories.summary: The Iran war is risking America’s global security ties and damaging its reputation, especially among the world’s Muslims, according to a set of State Department cables obtained by POLITICO. The cables, dated Wednesday, described the fallout of the war for America’s standing in three countries in different parts of the world: Bahrain, Azerbaijan and Indonesia. U.S. diplomats at embassies in the countries’ capitals painted damning portraits of an America under siege in multiple media spheres by pr

stories.title: Pentagon says Ukraine support can’t rely on American contributions
stories.summary: The Pentagon’s top policy official is warning that future military support for Ukraine cannot depend on the United States, sharpening pressure on European allies to take the lead. In remarks delivered at this week’s Ukraine Defense Contact Group meeting in Berlin and published on X on Thursday evening, Elbridge Colby said Washington’s support had relied heavily on “drawing down finite U.S. stockpiles,” an approach he signaled is no longer sustainable. “Europe must accelerate its assumption of pr

stories.title: US tech firms successfully lobbied EU to keep datacentre emissions secret
stories.summary: Legally questionable confidentiality clause adopted almost word for word from demands of Microsoft and trade groups Microsoft and other US tech companies successfully lobbied the EU to hide the environmental toll of their datacentres, an investigation has found, with demands to block a database of green metrics from public view written almost word for word into EU rules. The secrecy provision, which the European Commission added to its proposal almost verbatim after industry lobbying in 2024, hi

stories.title: Ben Roberts-Smith’s comrades say he ordered them to execute unarmed civilians, court documents show
stories.summary: Former SAS corporal allegedly placed man on his knees and ordered fellow soldier to shoot him, according to statement of facts Australian soldiers have told prosecutors they executed unarmed civilians at the orders of Ben Roberts-Smith or in complicity with him, according to a statement of facts tendered to the New South Wales local court. Roberts-Smith, a Victoria Cross recipient and once one of Australia’s most lionised soldiers, faces five charges of the war crime of murder, allegedly committ

stories.title: Slump in voters’ support for Israel shakes US consensus over military aid
stories.summary: Bipartisan backing for special relationship is fraying as Middle East conflicts turn public opinion Israel’s conflicts in the Middle East have driven a sea change in US public opinion, threatening a bipartisan consensus of support for military aid for Israel that has been the status quo for decades. In public opinion polling of Americans, among likely candidates for president, and even in pro-Israel lobbying circles, the special relationship enjoyed by Israel with the US is now under fire as hum

stories.title: Sanctions don’t result in regime change. Whether against Iran or Russia, western countries need shrewder tactics | Simon Jenkins
stories.summary: Dissent cannot take hold in soil rendered barren by emigration and embargo. Political opposition needs academic and cultural exchange if it is to prosper The chancellor of the exchequer and the IMF agree . Britain’s economy is about to take its greatest hit for decades. This is collateral damage from the US’s war on Iran and the closing of the strait of Hormuz – and will be made worse by sanctioning Gulf oil exports . Britain has already been weakened by four years of sanctions against Russia ov

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran to fully open strait of Hormuz during ceasefire, foreign minister says
stories.summary: Abbas Araqchi says passage for all commercial vessels is declared completely open for the remaining period of the ceasefire In case you’re just joining us, here are the latest developments in the Middle East to bring you up to speed. It’s 9am in Beirut and Jerusalem, 9.30am in Tehran and 2am in Washington DC. A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon has come into effect , pausing fighting between Israel and Hezbollah that has killed more than 2,100 Lebanese people and displaced more than 2.

stories.title: Starmer says it is ‘staggering’ and ‘unforgivable’ he was not told Mandelson failed vetting – UK politics live
stories.summary: PM responds to Guardian revelations that Foreign Office overrode failed security vetting for former minister Olly Robbins forced out in Mandelson vetting row Jones repeatedly denied that the prime minister had given a misleading impression about what has happened and had “lost grip” of the situation. He told BBC Radio 4’s Today programme: I completely refute the suggestion the PM misled the public or the House of Commons. It’s very clear from his words he was reporting what he had been told and

stories.title: Lebanon truce takes effect as damaged Qasmiyeh bridge reopens
stories.summary: Lebanon and Israel entered a 10-day truce early Friday, as the army reopened a single lane on the damaged Qasmiyeh Bridge in the south after Israeli strikes hit the key crossing linking Tyre to the wider region.

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: €90bn EU loan for Ukraine to be released in second quarter
stories.summary: EU economy commissioner says Iran war is feeding Russia’s war machine; Trump condemns massive strikes on Ukraine. What we know on day 1,513 The EU expects to start releasing a new €90bn loan to Ukraine in the second quarter, the bloc’s economy chief told AFP on Thursday. The EU’s economy commissioner, Valdis Dombrovskis, was speaking on the sidelines of the International Monetary Fund and World Bank’s spring meetings, which brought finance ministers, central bankers and other leaders to Washingt

stories.title: Starmer ‘furious’ over Mandelson vetting failures as Labour critics pounce
stories.summary: LONDON — Keir Starmer on Friday said he was “absolutely furious” and insisted he was not informed that Peter Mandelson had failed internal government security vetting before he was appointed as ambassador to the United States. The prime minister, who is facing fresh despair in his own ranks over a row which continues to dog his premiership, told broadcasters he was angered at a “staggering” omission by the Whitehall machine. “That I wasn’t told that Peter Mandelson had fa

stories.title: Sánchez seeks to rally global left against rise of far right
stories.summary: Progressive leaders from around the world are gathering in Spain on Friday to coordinate a response to the rise of far right and nationalist movements from Europe to the U.S. — with Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez at the center of the push. More than 3,000 participants from over 100 organizations are convening in Barcelona for the launch of the “Global Progressive Mobilization,” a new initiative aimed at strengthening international cooperation among left-leaning parties and activists. The e

stories.title: Fuel prices fall despite Geelong refinery blaze as Albanese says facility production has slipped by 40%
stories.summary: Prime minister says Corio refinery fire will not push Australia into stage 3 restrictions, despite reduced production of petrol, diesel and aviation fuel Track Australia’s fuel prices, service station outages and shipments in charts Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese says petrol production has fallen 40% at one of Australia’s two remaining oil refineries but the damage caused by fire at the Geelong facility will not lead to fuel restrictions. stories.title: French widow, 86, flies home after ICE detention ordeal
stories.summary: Marie-Thérèse Ross was arrested on 1 April and held in a Louisiana facility by immigration officials A French woman in her eighties who was arrested and placed in a US immigration detention centre has flown home. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents detained Marie-Thérèse Ross in Alabama on 1 April after she overstayed her 90-day visa, according to the US Department of Homeland Security. The 86-year-old widow was being held at a federal immigration detention facility in Louisiana. Continue

stories.title: House approves short-term extension of surveillance law in blow to Republicans’ long-term plan – US politics live
stories.summary: The decision to extend a warrantless security law until 30 April came after 20 Republicans worked with House Democrats to defeat attempts to pass five-year and 18-month renewals Sign up for the Breaking News US email Fourteen months after Robert F Kennedy Jr was sworn in as US health secretary, the country’s prime public health agency over which he presides is in a state of disarray. Eighty per cent of the top director positions at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) stand vacan

stories.title: IMF, World Bank restore relations with Venezuela
stories.summary: The IMF and World Bank have resumed formal engagement with Venezuela. The move further legitimizes the Latin American country's interim government and paves the way for financial support.

stories.title: Will Bulgaria's election change the country's course?
stories.summary: Bulgarians will vote in their eighth parliamentary election in five years on April 19. Former President Rumen Radev is projected to win. Radev, who has a record of pro-Russian stances, is pledging to fight corruption.

stories.title: Nuba Mountains, a fragile refuge on Sudan's front line
stories.summary: Squeezed between Sudan and South Sudan, the self-governed Nuba Mountains are grappling with complex war dynamics while hosting hundreds of thousands of refugees they are scarcely able to support.

stories.title: Myanmar frees over 4,000 prisoners in annual tradition
stories.summary: Myanmar's new president says he wants stability and reconciliation in a nation torn apart by a military coup. Those to be released include former President Win Myint.

stories.title: Ankara pushes deeper economic ties with Astana ahead of Erdoğan visit
stories.summary: Astana and Ankara aim to boost trade, diversify exports and launch joint industrial projects ahead of Erdoğan’s state visit to Kazakhstan, setting a goal of $15 billion in trade volumes.

stories.title: Finance ministers and top bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model
stories.summary: Experts say Mythos potentially has an unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cyber-security weaknesses.

stories.title: Military operation against Hezbollah 'still not complete,' Israel's defence minister says
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump announced a 10-day ceasefire on Friday after a meeting between the Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors, the first direct diplomatic talks between the two countries in decades.

stories.title: Can anyone stop Jordan Bardella in France? A crowded field could gift the election to the far right
stories.summary: A long list of contenders want to be president in 2027. But with anti-establishment sentiment dominating the national mood, outsiders have the advantage Wanted: politician capable of appealing to the moderate right, centre and moderate left to beat hard-right populist Jordan Bardella in the run-off of France’s 2027 presidential election. The search began in earnest after last month’s municipal elections , in which the left held on to most big cities while conservatives or the far-right National

stories.title: Gunfire reported in Beirut as truce comes into effect – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Our live coverage continues here Iran has stopped all petrochemical exports to prioritise domestic supply and prevent shortages of raw materials, Reuters reported. The state-owned National Petrochemical Company ordered firms to suspend exports until further notice. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘How do I end a call?’: the elderly Japanese people determined to master smartphones
stories.summary: Elderly people take advantage of courses on how to navigate mobile devices and avoid ‘analogue isolation’ It’s not only young people whose gaze is fixed on tiny screens. But for these users in Tokyo, clicking and scrolling is anything but second nature. “I can’t deal with all of the apps that jump out at me,” says one. “How do I know if I’ve definitely ended a call?” asks another. Continue reading...

stories.title: Woman stranded in Dusseldorf after return UK flight blocked over Home Office admin error
stories.summary: Liza Tobay, who lives in UK, was told her settled status had been ‘red flagged’ after trying to make a connecting flight from Munich to Edinburgh A German woman has been separated from her two-year-old daughter in Edinburgh after a Home Office mistake left her stranded in Dusseldorf earlier this week. Liza Tobay, who has lived in the UK for 15 years, had taken her oldest child, a six-year-old boy, to visit his grandfather and some other relatives over Easter when confronted with what she said ap

stories.title: Albanese’s visits to key allies have borne early fruits of fuel and fertiliser but ‘resilience’ is on the budget agenda
stories.summary: The prime minister has weathered the crises – for now – and there is a growing recognition that Australia is too vulnerable to world events Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese’s fuel diplomacy tour of Asia has already started paying dividends, but the real test could still be to come. stories.title: Weather tracker: hail covers parts of Tunisia and Algeria like snow
stories.summary: Accumulations of up to 3cm deep reported as severe thunderstorms also bring heavy downpours to central Italy Severe thunderstorms have affected the Mediterranean this week. On Monday, a surface low-pressure system in the Mediterranean in conjunction with an upper air cut-off low, led to thunderstorms over north Africa. stories.title: Ben Roberts-Smith released from prison on bail after being charged with five counts of war crime murder
stories.summary: Former SAS corporal granted bail ahead of potential trial on charges relating to alleged killing of civilians in Afghanistan Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Ben Roberts-Smith has been granted bail under strict conditions while he awaits a potential trial on alleged war crimes. The Victoria Cross recipient, once Australia’s most lionised soldier, faces five charges of war crime murder over allegations he killed un

stories.title: Employees at first ever Starbucks store seek to unionize amid fight for contract
stories.summary: Store opened in 1971 in Seattle’s Pike Place Market joins growing unionization campaign across the coffee chain Workers at the historic first Starbucks store are seeking to unionize as the coffee retail giant and its union appear stalemated over their first contract. The first Starbucks store opened in 1971 in Seattle’s Pike Place Market, and the store serves as a tourist site in Seattle. Continue reading...

stories.title: Why did Michelin snub St Paul in its guide to the best restaurants in the Great Lakes region?
stories.summary: Critics warn smaller and immigrant-run restaurants risk being overlooked as city-funded deal shapes dining map When Michelin announced that it was expanding its world-renowned restaurant guide into the Great Lakes region of the United States, including Minneapolis , one prominent city was left off the map – Saint Paul , the state capital. Despite being just 11 miles apart, the second half of Minnesota’s “Twin Cities” was absent from the highly anticipated announcement. The omission has raised co

stories.title: BBC radio DJ Andy Kershaw dies aged 66
stories.summary: DJ spent almost three decades working for corporation, and was best known for Radio 1 show from 1985 to 2000 The broadcaster Andy Kershaw, who presented BBC Radio 1 for almost 15 years, has died at the age of 66, his family have told the BBC. The DJ spent almost three decades working for the corporation and was best known for his Radio 1 show, which ran from 1985 to 2000. Continue reading...

stories.title: Average new UK electric car price is now lower than petrol vehicles
stories.summary: Autotrader says average EV cost is £785 cheaper, in an important milestone in the move away from fossil fuels Business live – latest updates The price of new battery electric cars has fallen below petrol cars in the UK for the first time, according to the car sales website Autotrader, in a significant milestone in Britain’s transition away from fossil fuels. The average price of a new electric car listed on the website was £42,620, compared with £43,405 for a new petrol model – making the former

stories.title: Olly Robbins is just the latest: a guide to the high-profile exits under Starmer’s tenure
stories.summary: There has been a steady succession of departures by ministers and senior officials in the past two years The exit of the top civil servant Olly Robbins from the UK Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) is the latest departure of an official or minister under Keir Starmer’s two-year tenure as prime minister. Here we take a look at some of the most high-profile resignations since Starmer came to power. Continue reading...

stories.title: Keir Starmer says it is unforgivable he was not told Mandelson failed vetting
stories.summary: PM ‘furious’ as spokesperson insists Downing Street repeatedly sought facts of the case without being told UK politics live – latest updates Keir Starmer has said it was “unforgivable” that he was not told that Peter Mandelson had failed his security vetting before taking up his role as ambassador to Washington. The prime minister said he was “furious” about what had happened, as he insisted he had not known that security officials had initially recommended that Mandelson be denied clearance. Co

stories.title: Hegseth says US is ‘locked and loaded’ to finish job of destroying Iran energy grid
stories.summary: US defense secretary says Iran’s energy infrastructure is ‘not destroyed yet’ while also lambasting the media Iran’s energy infrastructure is “not destroyed yet” and the US is “locked and loaded” to finish the job, Pete Hegseth, the defense secretary, said on Thursday as he called many of the press corps gathered the moral equivalent of the Pharisees who conspired to destroy Jesus Christ. Hegseth’s comments from the Pentagon podium came as a naval blockade of Iranian ports began this week and he

stories.title: Roberts-Smith gets bail in Australia over Afghan war crimes
stories.summary: Australia’s most decorated living soldier has been granted bail after being charged with war crimes in Afghanistan. The case is among the highest-profile in the country.

stories.title: US military in Libya: Pursuing unity, or pressuring Russia?
stories.summary: For the first time, Libya is hosting the Flintlock military exercise and soldiers from both sides of the divided country are taking part. It's due to security concerns, economic interests and competition with Russia.

stories.title: India: Parliament votes on women's quota, delimitation amid opposition row
stories.summary: India's Modi government is eyeing a passage of key bills that could overhaul Parliament seats and pave the way for a women's quota. The opposition has reservations, casting doubt on the intentions behind the push.

stories.title: Overshadowed by Iran, Gaza stuck between war and peace
stories.summary: With the war in Iran, the conflict in Gaza has faded from international attention. Despite a six-month ceasefire, efforts to end the conflict have stalled.

stories.title: Germany news: Far-right AfD ahead of Merz's conservatives
stories.summary: The far-right Alternative for Germany has nudged ahead of its political rivals in a new poll, the most recent of several that it has topped. Meanwhile, a last-ditch attempt to save whale Timmy is underway. Follow DW.

stories.title: Nueva Germania: The failed 'Aryan project' in Paraguay
stories.summary: A new home for the "Aryan race." That was what German emigrants envisioned when they founded Nueva Germania in Paraguay in 1886. It failed back then but a version of the town still exists today.

stories.title: Chess: Uzbekistan's new star shows Asia's continued rise
stories.summary: Javokhir Sindarov of Uzbekistan has earned the right to challenge India's Dommaraju Gukesh for the world title. Both men are under 21 and from Asia, underlining an ongoing demographic shift in top level chess.

stories.title: Nigeria: Students abducted enroute to Uni entrance exams
stories.summary: The governor did not specify the exact number of those kidnapped, but local media said it was 17. The incident comes amid a surge in violence in different parts of the country.

stories.title: Cyprus: Global politics felt at Candidates chess tournaments
stories.summary: The FIDE Candidates tournaments are two of the most important events in the chess calendar. This year, they took place in Cyprus. But tension relating to Russia and the war in the Middle East was never too far away.

stories.title: UK: 'Furious' PM Starmer refuses to step down over Mandelson
stories.summary: Pressure on Prime Minister Keir Starmer is growing over his naming of Peter Mandelson as US ambassador despite red flags. The PM says he was not told that Mandelson failed a background check before the appointment.

stories.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
stories.summary: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all the stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.

stories.title: Iran war: Temporary Israel-Lebanon ceasefire takes effect
stories.summary: As a 10-day ceasefire deal between Lebanon and Israel took effect, the Israeli military said its forces are going to remain in southern Lebanon. Iran says the Strait of Hormuz will reopen. DW has more.

stories.title: Several killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, many more injured
stories.summary: A 12-year-old was among those killed as Russian missile and drone attacks hit Ukrainian cities of Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro.

stories.title: Cuban president vows defense of island if US invades
stories.summary: Cuba's president has vowed the Caribbean island state will defend itself if the US launches a military assault. US President Trump has repeatedly threatened to invade.

stories.title: Artemis commander tells BBC about 'powerful' moment crater named after his late wife
stories.summary: Reid Wiseman’s two daughters were in Nasa’s mission control room for the naming of the “Carroll” crater in honour of the commander’s late wife.

stories.title: Runaway wolf finally captured after nine-day search in South Korea
stories.summary: The search has been marked by twists and turns, gripping South Korea and even inspiring a meme coin.

stories.title: US-brokered 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon goes into effect
stories.summary: Trump extended an invitation to Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to hold the first direct meeting at that level in decades.

stories.title: Moment wolf on the run in South Korea is found
stories.summary: The escape of Neukgu, a two-year-old wolf, from a zoo in the city of Daejon captured national attention.

stories.title: 'Ripple effects of Iran war could lead to other conflicts,' UN development chief tells Euronews
stories.summary: The head of the UN Development Programme told Euronews that the impact of the Middle East conflict is being felt "way beyond the region", and that, in the absence of a resolution, the groundwork for further conflicts is being laid.

stories.title: Cameroon sees mass turnout for Pope Leo XIV visit as he calls for peace
stories.summary: Cameroon saw thousands of faithful gather on Wednesday 16 April at Bamenda’s airfield, where Pope Leo XIV celebrated an open‑air Mass and called for a “decisive change of course” towards peace.

stories.title: Netherlands Keukenhof draws global visitors for spring display
stories.summary: Netherlands opened its Keukenhof garden in Lisse for the 2026 spring season, welcoming visitors to an eight-week display featuring around seven million flowers.

stories.title: Singer D4vd arrested on suspicion of murdering teenage girl
stories.summary: The remains of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez were found in the singer's car last year.

stories.title: Three charged with arson on Persian media offices in London
stories.summary: Two teenagers and a 21-year-old man are due in court charged with arson with intent to endanger life.

stories.title: Escaped wolf Neukgu captured after nine-day search in South Korea
stories.summary: A two-year-old wolf that escaped from O-World zoo in Daejeon, South Korea, has been safely recaptured after spending nine days on the loose. The male wolf, Neukgu, was born at the zoo in 2024 and is part of an initiative to restore wolves resembling those once native to the Korean peninsula.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: China and Russia ties driving North Korean economic recovery, Seoul ministry says
stories.summary: China has long been the diplomatically isolated country's main economic backer, though Pyongyang has also drawn closer to Moscow since it launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

stories.title: BBC in Iran: 'Tehran does not think it has lost this war'
stories.summary: Lyse Doucet says Iranians want a solution to the long-running animosity with the US, but leaders is not willing to make a deal on Washington's terms.

stories.title: Three charged over attempted arson on Persian-language TV channel, UK police say
stories.summary: Iran International is one of several Persian‑language outlets critical of Iran's clerical leadership operating from abroad.

stories.title: Germany’s Affordable Art Fair draws crowds with artworks under €500
stories.summary: Germany is hosting the Affordable Art Fair Berlin from 16 to 19 April 2026. The event brings together dozens of galleries and focuses on artworks priced under €500, aiming to broaden access to contemporary art.

stories.title: Nearly 900 Rohingya refugees dead and missing in shipwrecks in 2025, UNHCR says
stories.summary: The Rohingya mainly leave from huge camps in Bangladesh, where more than 1 million refugees forced to flee across the border from Myanmar's Rakhine state live in squalid conditions.

stories.title: Italy: 25 hostages freed as Naples bank gang vanishes underground
stories.summary: A group of three masked robbers held 25 people hostage during a bank heist in Naples on Thursday 16 April 2026 before escaping through a tunnel connected to the city’s sewer network, authorities said.

stories.title: ‘Offline Day’: Emmanuel Macron promotes books and theatre as alternatives to screens
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron met with pupils in Villers-Cotterêts to promote a new “Offline Day” initiative aimed at reducing screen time among young people. The visit took place at the Cité internationale de la langue française, where students participated in reading and theatre workshops.

stories.title: Pope criticises 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars after Trump spat
stories.summary: The comments follow a high-profile spat with US President Donald Trump, who called the Pope weak on crime.

stories.title: 'How does one survive?': Factory protests expose strain in India's industrial system
stories.summary: Workers in some north Indian cities have been protesting, demanding better pay and working conditions.

stories.title: Germany, the UK warn the Iran war distracts from Ukraine and oil price rises help Russia
stories.summary: Germany's defense minister warned that Russia "benefits from current developments in the Middle East," as the Ukraine Defense Contact Group met in Berlin. He said rising oil prices were filling Moscow's "war coffers."

stories.title: US blockade of Iranian ports to last 'as long as it takes,' Defence Secretary Hegseth says
stories.summary: The US naval blockade of Iran’s ports came into effect on Monday, with President Donald Trump warning that any attack ships would be “eliminated” if they attempted to break it.

stories.title: Israel escalates attacks on medics in Lebanon with deadly ‘quadruple tap’
stories.summary: Lebanese health ministry says killing of 91 healthcare workers shows ‘total disregard’ for international law Middle East crisis – live updates When they received the call to respond to an Israeli airstrike in the city of Mayfadoun, in southern Lebanon, most of the paramedics held back, having previously seen colleagues killed by double-tap attacks targeting rescuers. But the medics from the Islamic Health Association (IHA) rushed to the scene. By the time the other emergency workers arrived at t

stories.title: Armed robbers hold 25 people hostage at Naples bank before fleeing through hole in floor
stories.summary: Thieves believed to have escaped into sewers after holding staff and customers in Crédit Agricole branch for two hours Armed robbers held 25 people hostage at a bank in Naples for two hours on Thursday, before fleeing through a tunnel. The three thieves entered a branch of Crédit Agricole in the southern Italian city at about 11.30am, taking hostage staff and customers, who were freed by police a couple of hours later. Continue reading...

stories.title: Anatomy of the clitoris: 3D images reveal its nerve system
stories.summary: New 3D images reveal the clitoris’s complex nerve network, highlighting long‑standing gaps in medical understanding of the female body.

stories.title: Senegal: The Goethe-Institut opens its new building in Dakar
stories.summary: The Goethe-Institut's new building in Dakar champions sustainable architecture, made from the earth. Star architect Francis Kere has blended local materials with contemporary design.

stories.title: Pope Leo excoriates 'tyrants' waging war instead of peace
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV has admonished those waging war in the name of god and slammed "tyrants" destroying the world and its resources. The pontiff has stepped up his criticism of senseless violence, angering US President Trump.

stories.title: 10-day ceasefire between Israel, Lebanon goes into effect
stories.summary: The temporary ceasefire came after US President Trump spoke with Lebanese President Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu. Hezbollah says its commitment to the truce depends on Israel stopping attacks.

stories.title: Russia agrees to pay compensation to Azerbaijan over 2024 airliner tragedy
stories.summary: Russia and Azerbaijan have revealed that an "appropriate settlement of consequences," including compensation payment, has been reached over the Azerbaijan Airlines airliner crash in December 2024.

stories.title: Russia launches deadliest aerial attack in months, killing 18 in Ukraine
stories.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack killed two people in Russia, Moscow says, after an Orthodox Easter truce.

stories.title: Iran to blame for closing Strait of Hormuz, says Gulf leader
stories.summary: The Gulf Chief Jasem Mohamed AlBudaiwi told Euronews that Iran is responsible for weaponising the Strait to score a geopolitical win and should deal with the effects.

stories.title: Turkish police detain 162 people over online posts about school shootings
stories.summary: At least 16 people were injured in a shooting at a high school on Tuesday, before another nine were killed in a separate school shooting on Wednesday.

stories.title: Watch: Rising prices threaten Minnesota's meat raffles
stories.summary: Popular in local bars across the US, the games are facing higher meat prices, squeezing some of the charities that benefit from them.

stories.title: Ex-Virginia Lt. Governor kills wife and himself in murder-suicide, police say
stories.summary: Investigators say Justin Fairfax shot his wife, Cerina, multiple times before turning the gun on himself.

stories.title: Pakistan PM Sharif visits Doha as regional mediation gathers pace for US–Iran talks
stories.summary: With the fragile ceasefire holding and signs of de-escalation emerging, Pakistan’s mediation efforts to revive US–Iran talks are gathering pace, reflecting a broader push to contain tensions across the Middle East.

stories.title: Watch: Giant guitar-shaped forest revealed from the air in Argentina
stories.summary: Aerial footage captures the scale of “Estancia La Guitarra,” a vast tree formation in southern Córdoba province, Argentina. Created by Pedro Martin Ureta in memory of his wife, the project began in 1979 using more than 7,000 cypress and eucalyptus trees.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire, Trump says in social media post
stories.summary: Trump had announced earlier on Thursday that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon would speak about halting the fighting, in what would have been the first direct conversation in more than 30 years.

stories.title: South African opposition figure Malema sentenced to five years in prison for firing a gun
stories.summary: Malema is appealing against the decision, which means he is not going to prison immediately.

stories.title: Naples bank robbers hold 25 people hostage then vanish through tunnel
stories.summary: The armed men reportedly evaded capture by escaping through the city's sewer system.

stories.title: Lawmakers clash with RFK Jr as he shifts focus away from vaccines
stories.summary: During a three-hour hearing, the US health secretary tried to focus on chronic disease while being pressed on vaccines.

stories.title: Rescuers to use air cushions in latest effort to save stranded whale
stories.summary: "Timmy" has been stranded in the Baltic Sea for weeks despite several attempts to free the ailing animal.

stories.title: Artemis II crew describes Moon mission and splashdown moment
stories.summary: The crew held its first press conference since splashing down from their historic 10-day trip.

stories.title: School shootings a new trauma for Turkey as nation mourns
stories.summary: An expert tells the BBC the attack in Kahramanmaras was a tragedy but "not a surprise".

stories.title: Trump's Lebanon ceasefire takes Israel by surprise
stories.summary: Few Israelis see this truce as a way out of the conflict with Hezbollah, the BBC's Lucy Williamson writes.

stories.title: 'I was tortured and lost my hand' - one student's struggle to get an education in Nigeria
stories.summary: The BBC speaks to a student who pushed for his toe print to be taken to verify his identity.

stories.summary: Az amerikai elnök reméli, hogy a „Hezbollah rendesen viselkedik majd”.

stories.summary: A 444 szerint csak a dolgozók egy kis részét tartják meg, mivel a választási vereséget követően megcsappant a cég finanszírozása.

stories.summary: According to the Tisza's fiscal policy expert, under favourable circumstances, it may be possible even sooner.

stories.title: Hivatalosan is azt javasolja a Mol vezetősége, hogy idén később fizessenek osztalékot
stories.summary: Osztalékkifizetés esetén az MCC 24,12 milliárd forintot kaphatna a tavalyi év után az osztalékból.

stories.title: A DK nyáron tisztújítást tart, június közepén választhatják meg Dobrev Klára utódját
stories.summary: A választás nem hivatalos végeredménye szerint 1,13 százalékot kapott a párt.

stories.title: Nagy Márton visszavonul a politikától
stories.summary: „A miniszterség egy hivatás, az élet feladása” – válaszolta a miniszter korábban arra a kérdésre, miért szállt meg Brüsszelben éjszakánként 700–800 ezer forintért.

stories.title: Eltörte egy jegyellenőr csuklóját a szegedi tram-train egyik utasa
stories.summary: A férfi így akarta visszavenni az igazoltatáshoz átadott személyi igazolványát.

stories.title: Ezekért érdemes moziba menni április 17-től április 24-ig
stories.summary: Itt a következő hét napra szóló Telex Diszpó.

stories.summary: Marihuánát és kristályt is találtak a több helyszínen tartott razzián, ha elítélik a házaspárt, akár életük végéig börtönben maradhatnak.

stories.title: Se burgonyából, se cukorból nem vagyunk önellátóak, itt az ideje átalakítani a mezőgazdaságot
stories.summary: A rossz vízgazdálkodási gyakorlat és a merev termelési szerkezet is veszélyezteti az önellátást, de az sem jó, ha akkugyárak használják fel az értékes vizet.

stories.title: Egyre inkább úgy tűnik, hogy az egyik cukorfajta nemcsak azért hizlal, mert pluszkalória-bevitelt jelent
stories.summary: Az üdítőitalokban is gyakran előforduló fruktóz a zsírtermelésben és -tárolásban is szerepet játszik, több anyagcsere-betegség kulcsa lehet.

stories.summary: A volt jegybankelnök fiát a választás eredményéről és az MNB-ügyről kérdeztük, írásban válaszolt.

stories.title: Kétszáznál több filmes támogatja azt a programot, ami teljes rendszerváltást vár el a Tisza-kormánytól a filmiparban
stories.summary: Rendezők, forgatókönyvírók és más szakmabeliek írták alá azt a petíciót, amelyben pontokba szedték, mit várnak el a leendő kulturális minisztériumtól. A támogatási rendszert és az SZFE-t is átalakítanák.

stories.title: Természetkárosító motorosokat tartóztattak fel a Bükki Nemzeti Parkban

stories.title: Nem tud csak úgy lemondani a szolidaritási adóról a Tisza-kormány

stories.title: US launches fifth strike on alleged Pacific drug boat in a week, killing three
stories.summary: Wednesday’s strike brings the total of those killed in US military strikes on alleged drug boats to at least 177 Three people were killed in a US strike on another alleged drug-trafficking boat, the fifth such deadly attack in as many days, military officials have announced. US southern command said it conducted “a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations” in the eastern Pacific, without naming the alleged group, in an X post. stories.title: 'Everything flew': Ukrainians describe Russian attack on apartment block – video
stories.summary: Russian strikes killed at least 12 people in Ukraine , local authorities have said, after Moscow pummelled its neighbour during the night with drones and missiles. In Odesa, Russian strikes killed six people, according to the head of the city’s military administration. Strikes on the capital, Kyiv, killed at least four people, including a 12-year-old, the state emergency service of Ukraine said, while another two people died in the central city of Dnipro, according to Oleksandr Ganzha, the head

stories.title: Sheinbaum vows to ‘defend Mexicans at every level’ amid anger at Trump over migrant deaths
stories.summary: Sheinbaum has recently been taking a firmer stance with the US, defying pressures where other countries have caved The Mexican government has voiced concern about the deaths of its citizens in US custody, with Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum also pushing back against the Trump administration’s decision to impose an energy blockade on Cuba. The progressive Mexican leader has walked a careful line with Trump for more than a year, addressing provocations with a measured tone and meeting US requ

stories.title: Brazil's former spy chief released from ICE detention
stories.summary: Bolsonaro ally Alexandre Ramagem was stopped by immigration agents in Orlando, Florida on Monday.

stories.title: Repsol agrees Venezuela deal to boost oil production and regain control of assets
stories.summary: Spanish energy company Repsol has reached an agreement with Venezuela’s government and state-owned company Petróleos de Venezuela (PDVSA) to regain operational control of key oil assets and sharply increase production over the next three years.

stories.title: Short-term gains for China from US-Iran war may turn to longer-term pain
stories.summary: Beijing may be reaping some diplomatic benefit but Trump’s war holds risks for its energy security and economy Two months ago, China’s foreign minister, Wang Yi, promised it would be a “big year” for China-US relations. He was right, but perhaps not in the way he expected. Wang was speaking before a planned visit by the US president to Beijing in March, which would have been Donald Trump’s first trip to China since 2017. But the trip, and a meeting with his Chinese counterpart, Xi Jinping, was k

stories.title: Germany's aid to Ukraine faces challenges
stories.summary: At Ukrainian-German government talks, Berlin reassures Kyiv it can continue counting on military, civilian, and humanitarian aid, even as political opposition and US cuts create challenges for sustained support.

stories.title: Strikes hit Sloviansk and Sumy as Russia pounds Ukrainian cities
stories.summary: Russia strikes Sloviansk with a FAB-1500 bomb, wrecking civilian buildings, while repeated drone attacks ignite major fires in Sumy’s industrial zone.

stories.title: South Africa names apartheid-era negotiator as ambassador to US
stories.summary: Appointment of Roelf Meyer seen as attempt to improve relations amid false US accusations of ‘white genocide’ South Africa has appointed a former apartheid government chief negotiator during the talks that ended white rule in the 1990s as ambassador to the US, in what is seen as an attempt to improve the deeply strained diplomatic relationship between the two countries. Roelf Meyer replaces Ebrahim Rasool, who was expelled in March 2025 after he criticised the Trump administration. Continue read

stories.title: More than £1bn pledged for Sudan as humanitarian crisis deepens
stories.summary: Donors exceed funding target at Berlin conference but prospects for ceasefire remain distant More than £1bn (€1.15bn) has been pledged for war-ravaged Sudan at a conference in Berlin, eclipsing the funding target organisers had set to help mitigate the world’s largest humanitarian crisis. The financial commitments made on Wednesday will also help offset a chronic humanitarian funding shortfall in a country devastated by three years of conflict, where two-thirds of its population – 34m people – r

stories.title: How South Korea plans to use the Iran crisis to spur a renewables revolution
stories.summary: Energy crisis unfolding in Middle East has added political urgency, and more funding, to transform South Korea’s solar industry In Guyang-ri, a farming village of 70 households about 90 minutes south-east of Seoul, people gather for communal free lunches six days a week. The meals are funded by the village’s one-megawatt solar installation, which generates roughly 10m won ($6,800) in net profit each month. “Residents eat lunch together every day, so we see each other’s faces, talk together,” say

stories.title: ‘A dollar or two increase is devastating’: US consumers on toll of rising gas prices
stories.summary: Guardian readers describe how their lives have been upended by cost hikes stemming from Trump’s Iran war With the US and Israel’s war on Iran now in its seventh week, with a fragile ceasefire in place since earlier this month, Americans are continuing to feel the effects at the pump as global fuel prices rise . For several readers who spoke to the Guardian, the impact has forced difficult trade-offs – from accessing essential medicines and groceries to facing the brink of homelessness amid an al

stories.title: Pedro Pascal v Pedro Piscal: actor in legal battle with Chilean spirit brand
stories.summary: Pedro Piscal pisco is latest Chilean brand to resemble a Hollywood name – and others have fought off the lawsuits The actor Pedro Pascal is waging a legal battle against a Chilean pisco merchant who has chosen a cheeky name for his brand of the country’s national spirit: Pedro Piscal. David Herrera registered the brand name with a Chilean commercial regulator in 2023 and began selling his pisco in off-licences and restaurants. Continue reading...

stories.title: South African politician Julius Malema given five-year jail term for gun offence
stories.summary: Leader of leftwing Economic Freedom Fighters was convicted last year for firing rifle in the air at 2018 rally The South African leftwing politician Julius Malema has been sentenced to five years in prison for firing a rifle in the air at a political rally in 2018. Lawyers for the leader of the Economic Freedom Fighters, South Africa’s fourth largest political party, immediately appealed, and Malema will remain free while the appeal proceedings are under way. Continue reading...

stories.title: German health minister announces billions in cutbacks
stories.summary: The comprehensive reform package aims to plug a multibillion-euro hole in Germany's expensive health care system. Reforms include mandatory second opinions for costly surgeries and no more coverage for homeopathy.

stories.title: Berlin hosts Sudan conference amid brutal, forgotten war
stories.summary: The war in Sudan has been raging for the past three years, and a ceasefire is nowhere in sight. Participants at a conference of donors in Berlin hope at least to ease the suffering of the people caught in the middle.

stories.title: UK signs deal to rejoin EU's Erasmus exchange scheme
stories.summary: Britain will rejoin the popular Europe-wide student exchange program six years after leaving it.

stories.title: Germany news: Teacher group urges action over pupil violence
stories.summary: A German teachers' group says decisive action is needed to counter a surge in violence in classrooms and corridors. Meanwhile, the Greens say a blanket speed limit on major highways would help save fuel.

stories.title: US jury finds Live Nation, Ticketmaster hold illegal monopoly
stories.summary: The verdict will not bring immediate relief to concertgoers frustrated by high ticket prices, but could cost the company hundreds of millions of dollars.

stories.title: Germany-Israel divide deepens after Merz criticism
stories.summary: The Israeli finance minister's sharp criticism of the German chancellor's views on Israel's settlement policy is just the latest sign of a growing estrangement. What's the current state of German-Israeli relations?

stories.title: Top EU court rules online gamblers can sue for compensation if betting illegal in home country
stories.summary: The ECJ has ruled that online gamblers can seek compensation if they lost money when gambling was illegal in their home country, even if the laws have since changed. This could pave the way for others to reclaim losses.

stories.title: UN nuclear chief urges checks of Iran's programme in potential deal to end war
stories.summary: Tehran has not allowed the IAEA access to its nuclear facilities bombed by Israel and the US during a 12-day conflict in June, according to a confidential IAEA report.

stories.title: EU pursues 'deeper and stronger' partnerships with Gulf states, Costa tells Euronews in Doha
stories.summary: In the highest-level EU visit to the Gulf since the Iran war started, European Council President Antonio Costa reinforced the EU’s strategic partnerships with the Gulf states and pushed for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, warning of “disastrous consequences”.

stories.title: BBC to cut up to 2,000 jobs in next two years to save €575ml from operating costs
stories.summary: The BBC is funded by the public paying for a licence to view or listen to content and the corporation says 94% of UK adults use its services every month.

stories.title: Panicked scenes after school shooting in Turkey leaves at least nine dead
stories.summary: A 14-year-old armed with five guns opened fire at a Turkish school on Wednesday, killing at least nine people, wounding 13 others, the interior minister said.

stories.title: North Korea showing 'serious increase' in ability to make nuclear weapons, IAEA says
stories.summary: North Korea, which conducted its first nuclear test in 2006, is under rafts of UN sanctions for its banned weapons programmes.

stories.title: 'Discussions are being had' on second round of Iran talks in Pakistan, White House says
stories.summary: Negotiators are focusing on three key points that derailed last weekend's talks, including Iran's nuclear programme, the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for wartime damages.

stories.title: Back to books - Sweden's schools cutting back on digital learning
stories.summary: Swedish classrooms swap laptops for books, pens and paper, raising concerns from the tech sector.

stories.title: Ukraine: Deadly Russian strikes on Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro leave 12 dead
stories.summary: Russian strikes on Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro killed at least 12 people, including a child, as fighting and failed ceasefire efforts drag on in mid-April.

stories.title: Australia: Refinery fire raises fuel supply concerns amid Iran war
stories.summary: Fire at Viva Energy's Geelong refinery, which supplies 10% of Australia’s fuel, sparks supply fears as Iran war keeps global prices elevated.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV visits Cameroon as Biya faces unrest and separatist war
stories.summary: Arriving in Yaoundé on Wednesday, Pope Leo XIV pressed Cameroon’s leaders to address corruption, setting the tone for a high-stakes visit.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russian strikes hit Kyiv’s Podilsky district
stories.summary: Russian strikes hit Kyiv’s Podilsky district, injuring dozens and damaging homes, as part of a wider wave of deadly attacks across Ukraine.

stories.title: Spain: Sanchez government finalizes amnesty plan for undocumented migrants
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's government hailed the plan as "an act of justice and a necessity." It will allow unauthorized migrants to apply for temporary residence permits instead, and to start paying taxes.

stories.title: Germany: Man found guilty in Pelicot-style rape case
stories.summary: A Chinese student has been found guilty of drugging and raping a woman in Munich over a period of months. The perpetrator was a member of a Telegram chat group where men organized rapes and shared images of their crimes.

stories.title: Nine dead and 13 injured in second school shooting in Turkey this week, governor says
stories.summary: It comes a day after an ex-student opened fire at his former high school in Siverek in southern Şanlıurfa province, wounding 16 people including students before killing himself in a showdown with police.

stories.title: UK steel exports to EU at risk as bloc doubles tariffs and halves quotas
stories.summary: Decision to reduce duty-free quotas by 47% aimed at curbing Chinese imports The EU is to go ahead with plans to double tariffs and halve quotas on imports of steel from July, in a move designed to curb Chinese imports but which could damage UK exports to the bloc. The decision by EU lawmakers and member states after late night talks on Monday, will reduce duty-free quotas by 47%. Exact country allocations have yet to be determined. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK woman fears being stranded in Spain over new Home Office border rules
stories.summary: Natasha Cochrane de la Rosa was refused boarding on flight to London because she was not aware of the rule change A British woman has told how she fears being stranded in Spain for months after being refused boarding a on flight back home to London because she was not aware of new Home Office border rules. Natasha Cochrane de la Rosa, 26, was born in the UK to a British father and a Spanish mother, but because of archaic laws she was not entitled automatically to British citizenship because her

stories.title: Sudan: Iran war prompts 'massive' food, fuel price rises; drones kill hundreds of civilians in 2026
stories.summary: German charities warn of increases of 70-80% in the costs of essentials in wartorn Sudan amid the unrest in the Middle East. Donors gather in Berlin on Wednesday on the third anniversary of the outbreak of the war.
